# Pyodide core runtime snapshot

This directory vendors the browser-side core needed to bootstrap Pyodide for
SEBook's Python execution and AST-analysis workers.

- Package: `pyodide@0.27.0`
- Source archive: `https://registry.npmjs.org/pyodide/-/pyodide-0.27.0.tgz`
- npm archive integrity: `sha512-0O992noCKqv8lPw4+QFWw8d8yrNWVtQ6zPhWNg/RNYbFohiwmV6SGlVh5fWk/4pOxyhgHbayq+ur8JoR/r5U9A==`
- Runtime root: this directory

The local core consists of `pyodide.js`, `pyodide.asm.js`,
`pyodide.asm.wasm`, `python_stdlib.zip`, and `pyodide-lock.json`. Both workers
load the versioned script from this repository and pass this same-origin
directory to `loadPyodide({ indexURL })`, so none of the core bootstrap files
come from a CDN at runtime.

The same directory also contains the complete lockfile dependency closures for
`pytest` and `hypothesis`, which repository tutorials load during setup, plus
the exact `pyflakes` wheel used by live diagnostics. Pyodide checks each wheel
against the SHA-256 value in `pyodide-lock.json` before installing it. Other
Pyodide packages are intentionally not mirrored; a tutorial that introduces a
new `loadPackage()` dependency must vendor that package's complete closure in
the same change.

`pyodide-lock.upstream.json` is the untouched npm-package lock.
`package-overrides.json` records the reviewed pure-Python additions absent from
Pyodide 0.27.0:

- `hypothesis==6.125.3`, from its exact PyPI wheel, SHA-256
  `ae0381987d0ccacc62867343acc38a0ca5e959fc4da98b3e018b0debf080a557`,
  licensed MPL-2.0. Its wheel includes the upstream license metadata.
- `pyflakes==3.2.0`, from its exact PyPI wheel, SHA-256
  `84b5be138a2dfbb40689ca07e2152deb896a65c3a3e24c251c5c62489568074a`,
  licensed MIT. Its wheel includes the upstream license metadata.

The served `pyodide-lock.json` is generated by merging the upstream lock and
those reviewed overrides:

```sh
jq -s '.[0] * {packages: (.[0].packages * .[1].packages)}' \
  pyodide-lock.upstream.json package-overrides.json > pyodide-lock.json
```

`LICENSE` is the Apache-2.0 license from the Pyodide 0.27.0 source tag.
`SHA256SUMS` covers this README, every vendored core, package, lock, and license
file; verify it from this directory with:

```sh
shasum -a 256 -c SHA256SUMS
```

When upgrading, replace all core files from one exact npm archive, refresh the
tagged license, rebuild and verify every locally required package closure,
regenerate `SHA256SUMS`, update both workers together, and run `node --test
scripts/tests/runtime-supply-chain.test.js` plus the Pyodide tutorial browser
tests.
